step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture automates migrations from on-premises to AWS using a Migration Factory. 1. App Discovery Service gathers metadata from On-Prem. 2. CloudEndure continuously replicates block data to an EC2 hot replica. 3. The migration team inputs target metadata (tags, VPC, subnets) into DynamoDB. 4. The migration is triggered via Slack, calling the EC2 Migration Factory instance. 5. This controller pulls discovery metadata and target configs, stops replication, bakes an AMI from the replica, shares it to the target account, and launches the target EC2 instance.

graph.name: Automated Migration Factory with CloudEndure and AWS ADS
graph.notes: Reduces server migration time from 4 hours to 10 minutes by automating metadata aggregation, live block replication, and final target-instance orchestration.

nodes.name: Migration Team
nodes.notes: Orchestrates migrations and manages metadata config.

nodes.name: AWS Application Discovery Service
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Collects inbound/outbound TCP connections and utilization data.

nodes.name: Metadata Store
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores RPOs, RTOs, IPs, and tags for target environments.

nodes.name: Slack
nodes.notes: Chat interface used by migration team to trigger automation.

nodes.name: Migration Factory Controller
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Orchestrator EC2 instance driving APIs to coordinate migration phases.

nodes.name: Hot Replica Instance
nodes.notes: Staging server in replication area receiving continuous blocks.

nodes.name: Target EC2 Instance
nodes.notes: Final production server launched inside target account.

edges.notes: Sends TCP metadata and resource usage.
Writes replicated blocks to replication EC2 storage.
Reads target specs (tags, IPs, target account).
Gathers port metadata for firewall configurations.
Commands to bake replication volume into AMI.
Shares encrypted AMI and provisions EC2 in target account with sizing and tags.
